what_it_does: A token-driven CSS stylesheet for ViIdentity, a large overlay application surface (.id-panel) hosted inside the ViMemory overlay. It adds the idy-* shell and projection styles: header, rail navigation, aspect detail area, persistent Vi conversation column, network graph, composed-mind view, facets, awareness, recall, dials, kinds, streams, actions, behaviours with toggles, an interactive console/teach UI, generated-candidate cards with provenance trace and approval/rejection controls, lean-preference displays, and a home overview dashboard. It intentionally reuses mem-*, dsys-spec/dspec-*, and .sp classes from other stylesheets rather than redefining them.
observations: The entire file is namespaced under the idy-* prefix and uses BEM-style .idy-block__element--modifier class names.
Layout relies heavily on flexbox and CSS grid, with a fixed 248px rail, a fluid detail area, and a fixed 340px persistent Vi column.
A single responsive breakpoint at max-width: 920px collapses the three-column layout into a stacked, full-screen mobile view.
The persistent Vi column (.idy-vi) contains its own console thread, quick-action chips, and text input, styled in a gold-accented surface.
A network graph is rendered with absolute-positioned SVG dashed lines and nodes, used both as a small rail hero and a larger overview visualization.
Many interactive states are styled: hover, .on/active, disabled, approved/rejected, wait/error, and inline-editable fields with pen icons and textarea editors.
The console UI distinguishes Vi messages (bottom-left radius) from user messages (bottom-right radius, gold-50 background).
Generated candidate cards include a provenance trace (.idy-trace) with source tags such as preference, explicit, memory, ai, and default, plus lean chips.
Teaching UI includes kind selection pills, a scope select, a free-form text area, and a recent teachings list with remove buttons.
The home overview (.idy-ov) combines a network intro card, stat tiles, lean pills, recent artifacts, and primary action buttons.
The file references many CSS custom properties that are not defined locally (colors, spacing, radii, status tokens, shadows, easing).
The file ends mid-rule: the .idy-art selector is truncated after border: 1px solid var(--border-faint).
standouts: A persistent AI conversation column (.idy-vi) is integrated alongside every identity aspect, making chat part of the configuration surface.
Provenance trace UI (.idy-trace) surfaces why each generated candidate was produced, with source tags and positive/avoid lean chips.
Inline editable disposition text, leans, and full CRUD for behaviours/recall turn the identity panel into a direct-manipulation editor.
The network graph appears as both a decorative rail hero and a live overview visualization of identity nodes.
conventions: CSS custom properties with local fallbacks, e.g. var(--fs-xl, 20px).
Semantic token usage for color (accent-gold, accent-bronze, status-success, ink, fg-muted) and spacing (var(--s-*)).
Reuses external component class names (mem-*, dsys-spec/dspec-*, .sp) instead of duplicating their styles.
State expressed via modifier classes (.on, .approved, .rejected, .fresh, .off) rather than attribute selectors.
concerns: The file is truncated; the final .idy-art rule is incomplete.
Many referenced CSS custom properties are not defined in this file, so it depends on a global token stylesheet that is not shown.
Several magic dimensions (248px, 340px, 920px, 78px, 84px) appear without explanatory comments.
Some utility patterns (mini buttons, tags, chips) are repeated, which could drift from a shared design system if not consolidated.
Long class-name chains and many near-duplicate hover/active states make the stylesheet large and potentially brittle to token changes.
notes: A dense, gold-themed CSS projection layer that turns the ViIdentity overlay into an editable dashboard plus conversational control surface.
questions: Which JavaScript components mount these classes and toggle state classes like .on, .approved, .rejected, and .fresh?
Is .id-panel always rendered inside a ViMemory overlay, and how do the two overlays coordinate z-index and focus?
Where are the reused classes mem-*, dsys-spec/dspec-*, and .sp actually defined?
What was the intended completion of the truncated .idy-art rule?
Is the network graph purely decorative CSS/SVG, or is it data-driven by the identity model?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is exactly a token-driven CSS shell and projection layer for the ViIdentity overlay, adding idy-* styles while reusing external component classes as described.
apparent_themes: Identity as an inspectable, configurable mirror of the AI counterpart.
Transparency through provenance and lean-preference visibility.
Co-presence: conversation and configuration share the same surface.
Teachability: inline editing, CRUD, and scoped teaching flows.
Warm, dense dashboard aesthetic using gold/bronze accent tokens.
intention_signals: The stylesheet appears designed to support a user-facing identity mirror where a person can browse, edit, teach, and converse with their AI counterpart's preferences, memory, awareness, and generated artifacts, all within a single overlay.
novelty: The unusual combination of an identity-configuration dashboard, real-time provenance trace for generated candidates, and a persistent conversational column embedded in the same overlay surface.
contribution: A rich, token-driven CSS vocabulary for a large overlay identity application, including layout primitives, interactive state styles, a console/teach/produce flow, network visualization, and an overview dashboard, which a fused system could keep as the presentation layer for an inspectable AI identity.
summary_for_embedding: A token-driven CSS stylesheet defining layout, components, interactive states, console, teaching, generation trace, and overview dashboard for the ViIdentity overlay application inside ViMemory.
intention_for_embedding: To style a user-facing identity mirror where a person can browse, edit, teach, and converse with their AI counterpart's preferences, memory, and generated outputs.
